keyPoints: Testing dopamine-nutrition-label cues in AI-tool workflows (explicit cost framing, delayed gratification prompts, pause signals).
Ground experiments in JITAI/AIM-ACT for adaptive, context-sensitive delivery.
Measuring attention, distraction, and usage persistence as primary outcomes, with task type and cognitive load as moderators.
Prioritizing pause signals as pilot cue before adding cost framing and delayed gratification prompts.
Designing with autonomy-first, opt-out options and privacy-by-design principles.
Pre-registration, effect-size reporting, and heterogeneity analyses to map subgroups.
Translating findings into concrete design heuristics and knowledge-work protocols for responsible post-AI culture.
